steps.title: Set up using the rectangle perimeter formula
steps.content:
The perimeter of a rectangle is:
$$P = 2(\text{length}) + 2(\text{width})$$
With length $= x$ cm and width $= w$ cm (unknown), the perimeter is:
$$P = 2x + 2w$$
Since the function $f(x) = 2x + 186$ represents this perimeter, we have:
$$2x + 2w = 2x + 186$$

steps.title: Solve for the width
steps.content:
Both sides of the equation contain $2x$, so the constant terms must be equal:
$$2w = 186$$
Dividing both sides by 2:
$$w = 93$$
That is the correct answer.
